title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
Accidental Death Benefit In Term Insurance
Death remains an unpredictable fact to every human. Hence, you can only work towards a better tomorrow for yourself and your family and be prepared for the worst. The passing away of any family member is disheartening, and the case becomes even more serious if the only earner of the family leaves. Due to the widespread recognition of this sorrow, there are certain insurance plans specifically designed to ensure financial stability for the family of the insured in case of their unfortunate demise. A term insurance plan is one such plan. If a term insurance plan is not yet a part of your policy portfolio, invest in one today without any delay.
As the name suggests, a term insurance plan is a pure life cover plan for a specific tenure. If the insured passes away during the policy tenure, the family/nominee of the insured receives a death benefit. One of the widely asked questions is, "does term life insurance cover accidental death". The answer to this question is, Yes. A range of causes of death are included in the term insurance plan, and accidental death is one of them. So, if you are wondering is accidental death covered in term insurance, let us know more about the plan.
Does term insurance cover accidental death?
Accidents as a cause of death are covered in term insurance plans. However, did you know that you can purchase riders as well to increase the death benefit of your plan? The option of rider or add-on is given so you can widen the benefit of an insurance plan. However, you can note that an increase in the coverage of the plan also leads to an increase in the premium. Though the premium is quite affordable as compared to the coverage it offers, it is essential that you first plan your budget and then go for an add-on.
The add-on of accidental term insurance increases the overall death benefit of the plan. It provides coverage for accidental death along with partial and total disability cover¹. Let's understand it with the help of an illustration:
Ravish, a 35-year-old purchased a term insurance plan (without add-on) for 20 years tenure. After 5 years of buying the plan, Ravish passes away in a motor accident. The family receives the assured death benefit of Rs 40 lakhs.
Meera, a 40-year-old, bought a term insurance plan for 15 years that offers a death benefit of Rs 40 lakh. Meera also decides to purchase the add-on accidental term insurance along with her base term insurance plan. The add-on offers an additional death benefit of Rs 15 lakhs. After 10 years of purchasing the plan, Meera has a car accident and passes away. The family behind receives a total death benefit of Rs 55 lakhs (base death benefit plus add-on benefit). If this add-on was not opted for, then the death benefit would have been only Rs 40 lakhs².
This is how the add-on benefits the family if the insured passes away in an accident. The base plan of term insurance covers accidents as a cause of death. However, if you purchase this add-on along with the plan, it gives you the leverage of an extended benefit.
Additionally, the accidental death benefit rider premiums and the benefit paid out are also eligible for tax rebates under Section 80C of the Income Tax Act, 1961, as per the provisions stated therein3. Also, the sum assured received under the rider is exempt from tax, under section 10(10D) of the Income Tax Act, 1961, as per the provisions stated therein4.
Conclusion
Accidents as a cause of death have become a major and rising concern. It is not uncommon to hear and read news of accidents. Now that your question ‘does term life insurance cover accidental death’ is answered your question, you need not panic and worry. Nobody knows tomorrow, but you can definitely keep your guard on for any unforeseen and unfortunate events. Purchasing a term plan with sufficient and the right cover is one such plan. If you haven't invested in a term insurance plan yet, now is the right time.
Disclaimer:
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale. full_pdf_text:
Accidental Death Benefit in a Term Insurance
Death remains an unpredictable fact for every human. Hence, you can only work towards a better tomorrow for yourself and your family and be prepared for the worst. The passing away of any family member is disheartening, and the case becomes even more serious if the only earning member of the family passes away. Due to the widespread recognition of this sorrow, there are certain life insurance plans designed to ensure financial stability for the family of the life insured in case of the life insured person’s unfortunate demise. A term insurance plan is one such plan. If a term insurance plan is not yet a part of your policy portfolio, you may consider buying one today. As the name suggests, a term insurance plan is a pure life cover plan for a defined tenure. If the life insured passes away during the policy tenure, the nominee of the life insured receives a death benefit. One of the widely asked questions is, "does a term life insurance cover accidental death". The answer to this question is, Yes. A range of causes of death are included in the term insurance plan, and accidental death is one of them. Let us know more about the plan. Does term insurance cover accidental death? Accidents as a cause of death are covered in term insurance plans. However, did you know that you can purchase riders as well to increase the death benefit of your life insurance plan with nominal extra premium? The option of rider or add-on may be bought additionally, so that you can widen the scope of a life insurance plan. However, to avail additional coverage benefits, the premium may also increase. Though the premium is quite affordable as compared to the coverage it offers, it is essential that you plan your budget and need before buying an add-on. Riders, if taken with term insurance, increase the overall death benefit of the life insurance plan. Riders like the accidental permanent partial/total disability rider, provides coverage for accidental death along with partial and total disability cover¹. Let's understand it with the help of an illustration: Ravish, a 35-year-old purchased a term insurance plan (without riders) for 20 years tenure for a sum assured of Rs. 40 lakhs. After 5 years of buying the plan, Ravish passes away in a motor accident. The family receives the assured death benefit of Rs 40 lakh under the policy, presuming all due premiums have been paid. Meera, a 40-year-old, bought a term insurance plan for 15 years that offers a death benefit of Rs 40 lakh. Meera also decides to opt for the accidental death benefit rider with nominal extra cost along with her base term insurance plan, that offers an additional rider benefit of Rs. 15 lakh. After 10 years of purchasing the plan, Meera passes away in a car accident. The nominee receives a total death benefit of Rs 55 lakhs (base death benefit plus rider benefit). If this rider was not opted for, then the death benefit would have been only Rs 40 lakh.
The base plan of term insurance covers accidents as a cause of death. However, if you purchase rider along with the plan, it gives you the leverage of an extended benefit. Additionally, the accidental death benefit rider premiums and the benefit paid out are also eligible for tax rebates under Section 80C of the Income Tax Act, 1961 1. Also, the sum assured received under the rider is exempt from tax, under section 10(10D) of the Income Tax Act, 1961. However the deductions and exemptions mentioned above are subject to provisions stated in the Act.2 Conclusion Accidents as a cause of death have become a rising concern. It is not uncommon to hear and read news of accidents. Now that your question ‘does term life insurance cover accidental death’ is answered, you need not panic and worry. Nobody knows about tomorrow, but one can definitely keep their guards on for any unforeseen and unfortunate events that may happen in future. Purchasing a term plan with sufficient cover is one such safe guard. If you haven't purchased a term insurance plan yet, consider investing now. Disclaimer: The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure, rider brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale. Please check with your tax consultant for eligibility. 2https://incometaxindia.gov.in/communications/circular/circular-2-2022.pdf
